LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRYANT FU, CRYSTAL LEI'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING VEXATIOUS LITIGANT DEMAS YAN TO POST SECURITY (CCP 391 ET SEQ) IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 5, 2018 AT 9:30 AM. MR. LATHAM TO BE PRESENT IN COURT. SUPPLEMENTAL MOVING PAPERS DUE ON 5/11/18. SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION DUE ON 5/18/18. SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY DUE ON 5/22/18. SUPPLEMENTAL PAPERS ARE LIMITED TO THE ISSUE OF WHETHER MR. YAN IS A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT FOR THE PURPOSE OF FURNISHING SECURITY. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER RESONSIDERTAION OF ORDER REQUIRING DEMAS YAN TO POST SECURITY (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-30-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DEMAS YAN'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE JUNE 29, 2018 ORDER REQUIRING HIM TO POST SECURITY IS DENIED. NONE OF THE AUTHORITIES CITED BY MR. YAN SHOW THAT THE MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANTS BRYANT FU AND CRYSTAL LEI TO REQUIRE MR. YAN TO POST SECURITY WAS INCORRECTLY DECIDED. MR. YAN HAS NOT CITED ANY CALIFORNIA AUTHORITY, NOR IS THE COURT AWARE OF ANY SUCH AUTHORITY, THAT THE CONDUCT ALLEGEDLY COMMITTED BY MR. FU AND MS. LEI IS ACTIONABLE IN A CIVIL CASE. SIGNIFICANTLY, THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT EITHER MR. FU OR MS. LEI KNEW THAT TONY FU PLANNED TO COMMIT AN ACT OF VIOLENCE AGAINST MR. YAN OR THAT EITHER OF THEM WAS PRESENT WHEN THE ACT OF VIOLENCE OCCURRED OR TOOK ANY STEP THAT FACILITATED TONY FU IN COMMITTING AN ACT OF VIOLENCE AGAINST MR. YAN. AT MOST THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT MR. FU AND MS. LEI PARTICIPATED IN A COVER UP OF AN ACT OF VIOLENCE COMMITTED BY TONY FU SO THAT TONY FU COULD AVOID CRIMINAL LIABILITY. NO CASE OR STATUTE STATES THAT CIVIL LIABILITY LIES IN THESE CIRCUMSTANCES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

ORDER (GRANTING MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING VEXATIOUS LITIGANT DEMAS YAN TO POST SECURITY)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CRYSTAL LEI AND BRYANT FU'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER REQUIRING VEXAIOUS LITIGANT DEMAS YAN TO POST SECURITY IS GRANTED. PER CCP 391.3 AND 391.4, UNLESS MR. YAN FURNISHES ACCEPTABLE SECURITY IN THE AMOUNT OF $50,000 FOR THE BENEFIT OF MS. LEI AND MR. FU NO LATER THAN JULY 31, 2018, THIS CASE WILL BE DISMISSED AS TO MS. LEI AND MR. FU ON AUGUST 1, 2018. PER CCP 391.6, THIS CASE IS STAYED UNTIL JULY 31, 2018 OR THE DATE AS MR. YAN FURNISHES THE REQUIRED SECURITY, WHICHEVER OCCURS FIRST. MS. LEI AND MR. FU HAVE SHOWN THAT MR. YAN IS A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 391(B) AND THAT THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT MR. YAN WILL PREVAIL IN THIS CASE. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2018 Continued To Sep-05-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Requiring Demas Yan To Post Security
Off Calendar Jul-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Demas Yan's motion for reconsideration of the June 29, 2018 order requiring him to post security is denied. None of the authorities cited by Mr. Yan show that the motion filed by defendants Bryant Fu and Crystal Lei to require Mr. Yan to post security was incorrectly decided. Mr. Yan has not cited any California authority, nor is the court aware of any such authority, that the conduct allegedly committed by Mr. Fu and Ms. Lei is actionable in a civil case. Significantly, the complaint does not allege that either Mr. Fu or Ms. Lei knew that Tony Fu planned to commit an act of violence against Mr. Yan or that either of them was present when the act of violence occurred or took any step that facilitated Tony Fu in committing an act of violence against Mr. Yan. At most the complaint alleges that Mr. Fu and Ms. Lei participated in a cover up of an act of violence committed by Tony Fu so that Tony Fu could avoid criminal liability. No case or statute states that civil liability lies in these circumstances. Order signed. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

Defendants Crystal Lei And Bryant Fu'S Motion For An Order Requiring Vexaious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security
Off Calendar Jun-29-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Lapham, Esq. 415-867-5797 of Yan Law Offices for Plaintiff Demas Yan through Court Call.
Michael Johnson, Esq. 415-362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith for Defendant Crystal Lei And Bryant Fu

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court Denies plaintiff's request to amend the Complaint. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants' Crystal Lei And Bryant Fu'S Motion For An Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security (CCP section et seq.)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling that was issued prior to the June 26 hearing. Defendants Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu's motion for an order requiring vexatious litigant Demas Yan to post security is granted. Per CCP 391.3 and 391.4, unless Mr. Yan furnishes acceptable security in the amount of $50,000 for the benefit of Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu no later than July 31, 2018, this case will be dismissed as to Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu on August 1, 2018. Per CCP 391.6, this case is stayed until July 31, 2018 or the date as Mr. Yan furnishes the required security, whichever occurs first. Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu have shown that Mr. Yan is a vexatious litigant within the meaning of CCP 391(b) and that there is no reasonable possibility that Mr. Yan will prevail in this case. $50,000 is the minimum reasonable amount that Ms. Lei and Mr. Fu are likely to incur to defend themselves in this case. Although Mr. Yan is represented by an attorney in this case, that representation does not preclude Mr. Yan from the posting security requirement.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Off Calendar Jun-05-2018 Continued To Jun-26-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Mark Lapham, Tel. No. 1-925-837-5007 appearing for the Plaintiff Demas Yan;
Atty. Michael J. Johnson of Lewis Brisbois & Smith LLP, Tel. No.1-415-438-6648
appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - DEFENDANTS BRYANT FU AND CRYSTAL LEI'S Motion For An Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security is continued to June 26, 2018 to allow Mr. Yan to respond to the Flores/Camerado case which stands for the proposition that a vexatious litigant cannot avoid the posting of a security bond. Supplemental Brief to be filed by June 15, 2018. Short Reply consisting of 2 pages to be filed by June 21, 2018. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security
Off Calendar May-07-2018 Continued To Jun-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Latham, Esq. of Yan Law Offices 415-867-5797 for Plaintiff Demas Yan through Court Call.
Shoba Dandillaya, Esq. 415-362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois for Defendants BRYANT FU, CRYSTAL LEI.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court shares his views, concerns and issues. Defendant Argues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BRYANT FU, CRYSTAL LEI'S Motion For An Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant Demas Yan To Post Security (CCP 391 et seq).

Continued to June 5, 2018 at 9;30 am. Mr. Latham to be present in Court. Supplemental moving papers due on 5/11/18. Supplemental Opposition due on 5/18/18. Supplemental Reply due on 5/22/18. Supplemental papers are limited to the issue of whether Mr. Yan is a vexatious litigant for the purpose of furnishing security.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
